description:
**Problem**
Replay maps all SegmentHeader errors to segment_header_mismatch, including unsupported wal_format_version and header decode failures. REALTIME_ERRORS.md distinguishes wal_format_unsupported from semantic header mismatch and from corruption.

**Where**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs: SegmentHeader::decode reports unsupported wal format version
- src/daemon/ops.rs: wal_replay_error_code maps SegmentHeader to segment_header_mismatch
- src/daemon/ipc.rs: mirrored mapping

**Design**
- Add a specific error variant (or reason parsing) to detect wal_format_version mismatch and map to wal_format_unsupported with details {wal_format_version, supported}.
- Split decode failures (bad magic/CRC/truncated header) from semantic mismatch and map to wal_corrupt (or specific corruption code) instead of segment_header_mismatch.

**Acceptance**
- Unsupported wal_format_version yields wal_format_unsupported.
- Header decode failures yield corruption code, not segment_header_mismatch.
- True store_id/store_epoch mismatch continues to use segment_header_mismatch.

**Files**
- src/daemon/wal/segment.rs
- src/daemon/ops.rs
- src/daemon/ipc.rs
title: WAL replay: map wal_format_unsupported and header decode vs mismatch

acceptance_criteria:
**Acceptance**
- [ ] Repl ActorModel implements `Representative` and uses `checker().symmetry()`.
- [ ] State space is measurably smaller for >=3 replicas (documented in model output).
- [ ] No invariant regressions under symmetry.
description:
**Problem**
Replication models will blow up in state space without symmetry reduction. We are not using Stateright’s Representative/Rewrite system.

**Goal**
Add symmetry reduction to the ActorModel replication state to keep searches tractable without weakening invariants.
design:
**Design**
1) Implement `Representative` for the repl model state (or the auxiliary history type) to canonicalize actor identities.
2) Use `Rewrite`/`RewritePlan` to reindex per-actor maps/sets.
3) Validate that properties remain invariant under symmetry (no identity-sensitive checks unless intentionally pinned).
4) Turn on `checker().symmetry()` in the example when using symmetric network modes.

**References**
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/checker/representative.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/src/checker/rewrite.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/examples/2pc.rs`
- `~/.cargo/registry/src/index.crates.io-1949cf8c6b5b557f/stateright-0.31.0/examples/increment.rs`
title: Stateright repl: symmetry reduction

description: cargo clippy -p beads-rs -- -D warnings fails with manual checked division in cli/commands/{epic,show}.rs and unnecessary sort_by in cli/commands/show.rs, daemon/admin.rs, daemon/wal/memory_index.rs, git/checkpoint/cache.rs. Fix to restore clippy-clean baseline.
title: Fix clippy -D warnings in cli/show+epic and daemon/admin/wal/memory_index, git/checkpoint/cache

description: tests/integration/fixtures/load_gen.rs always spawns worker threads, even when workers=1 (common in tests). Add a single-thread fast path to avoid thread spawn + atomics/mutex overhead for small loads.
title: LoadGenerator spawns threads even for 1 worker; add fast path

acceptance_criteria:
- [ ] Command aliases are visible in help for commands that have them.
- [ ] Key commands such as create, update, list, show, dep, prime, and setup have nontrivial long help and examples.
- [ ] Blank or placeholder help text is removed from current command output.
- [ ] There is an explicit way to list commands and/or export command docs for agents and docs generation.
- [ ] Per-command help quality improves independently of the custom root help surface.
description:
**Problem**
beads-rs help is flat and underpowered compared with beads-go. It lacks grouped top-level help, visible alias sections, richer per-command descriptions/examples, and machine-friendly inventory/doc generation. Some current help output is actively weak, such as blank field descriptions in `bd update --help`.

**Files:** crates/beads-cli/src/cli.rs, crates/beads-cli/src/commands/**/*.rs, any help-template/render support introduced for clap
design: Port the strongest per-command help/discoverability patterns from beads-go after the custom root help slice lands: visible alias information, long descriptions/examples for key commands, and a command inventory/doc export surface analogous to `bd help --list` / `--all` / `--doc` or an equivalent clap-native design. Keep output intentional for both humans and agents. Root help grouping and bare `bd` / `bd help` presentation are tracked separately so this bead can stay focused on command-level help quality and discoverability beyond the root surface.
title: Overhaul CLI help and command discoverability
